# Agent Memory Bridge

Agent Memory Bridge is a local-first stdio MCP server for persistent project
memory that helps coding agents start with the right context.

Primary docs:

- README.md - product overview, install, first useful loop, public MCP tools
- INSTALL_FOR_AGENTS.md - agent-readable install protocol
- docs/INTEGRATIONS.md - client configuration examples
- docs/CONFIGURATION.md - runtime and classifier configuration
- docs/AUTHORITY-CONTRACT.md - source-of-truth and compiled-view rules
- docs/CONTEXT-ASSEMBLY.md - startup/task context assembly model
- docs/HARNESS-DESIGN.md - boundary between AMB Core and future harness tools
- benchmark/README.md - benchmark and proof snapshots
- SECURITY.md - local-first security model and reporting

Stable public MCP tools:

- store
- recall
- browse
- stats
- forget
- promote
- export
- claim_signal
- extend_signal_lease
- ack_signal

Important boundaries:

- No separate startup_packet, task_packet, or Task Brief MCP tools.
- No watcher, scheduler, autonomous runner, hosted backend, or graph database in
  AMB Core.
- Startup and task-time packets are compiled views over existing records.
- Optional helper commands can render Task Brief reports around the bridge;
  Task Brief is a derived CLI/report view, not part of the core MCP surface.

Useful commands:

```bash
agent-memory-bridge doctor
agent-memory-bridge verify
agent-memory-bridge first-run --client generic --example
agent-memory-bridge config --client generic --example
```
